dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: Fresh-run repro shows data loss/corruption: after `generate-reintegration`, running `rws update-summary test-row recommendations` changes the reintegration block by injecting `- New rec.` inside `<!-- reintegration:begin --> ... <!-- reintegration:end -->`, so the claimed round-trip behavior does not hold.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: `tests/integration/test-reintegration.sh` passes 44/44 but never exercises the failing path of generating reintegration and then updating a normal summary section, so the real round-trip regression is uncovered by tests.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: `rws get-reintegration-json` accepted a deliberately schema-invalid `reintegration.json` (bad `generated_at`, missing `files_changed[].category`, invalid `open_items[].urgency`, wrong `merge_hints` types) and exited 0, so the schema contract is not actually enforced.

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: The deliverable's `file_ownership` in `.furrow/rows/install-and-merge/definition.yaml` omits `bin/frw.d/scripts/generate-reintegration.sh`, `commands/merge.md`, and `bin/frw.d/scripts/merge-*.sh`, yet those files were modified and no ownership amendment/justification is recorded there.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: Shell quality is below project standard: `shellcheck` reports POSIX violations in `tests/integration/test-reintegration.sh` (`SC3001` process substitution under `#!/bin/sh`) and unsafe filename handling in `bin/frw.d/scripts/generate-reintegration.sh` (`SC2038`).
